_meta.updated: 2026-05-29 (Stage 3x.2g — Ale answers applied + CONFIRMED: tiered deposit €200 package / €100 custom-or-short-stay; non-7-night proration corrected to package_total/7 round-up-5 per night; rental prices CORRECTED (wetsuit €5, soft top €15, hard board €20) with bundle promos (wetsuit+softtop €15, wetsuit+hardboard €20 — wetsuit free); lesson tiered pricing (1 lesson €35, 2+ €30 each); yoga €15 booked on site (confirmed); lesson scheduling manual w/ two daily slots; bad-weather lesson refund staff day-by-day; refund default = 12-month voucher, cash refund exception, always staff. Generalizable behaviors (deposit tiers, add-on quantity tiers, bundles, voucher refund mechanism, lesson scheduling) promoted to engine-default mechanisms in _deploy-config.template.json; only VALUES live here.)
_meta.purpose: Stage 3x.2 minimum business logic baseline for Stage 4 entry. All rules are classified: confirmed / provisional / partially_confirmed / handoff_only / owner_required / nice_to_have. Rules marked owner_required must not be used as if known — safe defaults apply until Ale/Cami confirm. 3x.2c applied confirmed owner answers (payment-link auto-send, hold expiry, confirmation content, conditional cancel/date-change, rooming + operator-room logic). 3x.2d added WORKING/PROVISIONAL prices and policies (deposit €200, 2026 package table, proration, derived add-on prices, check-in/out times, cancellation/date-change conditions, handoff to WhatsApp, staff-directory password edit). Pricing is PROVISIONAL — see pricing_policy. A real autonomous charge to a real guest requires pricing_status=confirmed; provisional prices are safe for dry-run/shadow only.

secrets._note: 3x.2e: real phone numbers + admin password are NOT stored in this committed config. They live in an untracked secret file (gitignored). Anything referenced as 'secret:<key>' is resolved from that file. To update a number/password later, edit the secret file only.

deploy_config._note: PORTABILITY: this whole file IS the per-client DEPLOY CONFIG. The engine is generic and reads these values; nothing surf-house-specific is hardcoded in code/workflows. Deploying a NEW client = copy the template, fill these deploy-time values, create a secret file, (and for a different vertical) pick an inventory model. See docs/DEPLOYMENT-CONFIG.md and config/clients/_deploy-config.template.json.
deploy_time_fields: secrets (handoff number, master-admin numbers, admin password)
packages.* (catalog, seasons, prices, inclusions, room-type modifiers)
deploy_config._vertical_note: Surf shop / surf school reuse the same engine; they swap packages->offerings and rooming->slots/rentals inventory. See ROADMAP § Engine portability and the surf-*.sample.json paper tests.

pricing_policy._note: 3x.2d: governs how prices may be used. Every priced item carries pricing_status. PROVISIONAL prices (the working numbers below, supplied by the operator pending Ale/Cami sign-off) may be used in dry-run, golden tests, and Stage 3y shadow drafts (staff approve before send). They must NOT be used to autonomously charge or quote a real guest in live mode — that requires pricing_status=confirmed. Flip provisional -> confirmed when owner verifies; values are kept in single easy-to-edit fields.

payment._note: 3x.2c: bot may auto-send payment link once required details collected, asks deposit-vs-full, hold 6 hours, balance payable at arrival via cash/bank/stripe. 3x.2g (Ale): deposit is TIERED by booking type — standard package €200, custom pack / shorter stays €100. Encoded via the engine-default deposit_rule mechanism (tiered_by_booking_type); values are CONFIRMED by Ale.

deposit_rule._note: 3x.2g (Ale, CONFIRMED): deposit varies by booking type. Standard named-package booking = €200; a custom-built package or a shorter (non-7-night) stay = €100. Engine reads `type` + `tiers` + `default`; values are client config. To change later, edit the numbers here only.

custom_or_short_stay._match: custom-built package OR non-7-night / shorter stay

deposit_rule._default_note: fallback when booking type is ambiguous; bot should resolve type before quoting deposit, else handoff

confirmation._note: 3x.2c: owner approved auto-confirm AFTER payment truth, and confirmed content (address, gate code, room number; NOT bed number). IMPORTANT distinction: the auto-confirm DECISION is approved, but the actual REAL WhatsApp send is still a separate gate — keep dry_run / staff_approval send until Stage 3y shadow mode or a real-send gate proves it. confirmation_send_mode 'auto_after_payment_truth' describes the trigger logic, not authorization to send live WhatsApp yet.
confirmation.address: C. Mies de La Ran, 41, 39140 Somo, Cantabria

cancellation._note: 3x.2d: owner clarified. UNPAID bookings can be cancelled by the bot. PAID cancellations -> staff. ALL changes -> staff UNLESS the new booking has the SAME night/day count AND the SAME rate AND availability exists -> then the bot may move it. Refunds are always manual/staff. no_show_policy still owner_required.

refund_mechanism._note: 3x.2g (Ale, CONFIRMED): refunds are handled manually and 'it depends'. The DEFAULT remedy is a VOUCHER/credit, not cash. Engine-default mechanism (default_remedy = voucher | money), values per client. Bot never issues a refund or voucher itself — always staff.

voucher._scope_note: usable for a year here in Somo for the next surf trip

money_refund._note: rarely refund money; FULL money refund when a guest is genuinely unhappy / has a real legitimate reason

cancellation._no_show_note: 3x.2e: on no-show, keep the deposit and return the rest. Any actual refund of the remainder is executed manually by staff (default remedy = voucher per refund_mechanism).
cancellation.cancellation_safe_reply: I'll connect you with our team to handle this — please give us a moment.
cancellation.refund_safe_reply: Cancellations and refunds are handled by our team directly — they usually arrange a voucher you can use within a year. Let me pass you over.
cancellation.date_change_safe_reply: Date changes need to be confirmed by our team. I'll flag this for them now.

rooming._note: 3x.2c: owner approved bot auto-assign when Rooms table/config is clear. Airtable Rooms table is authoritative — config is guidance only. Bot must never move staff/manual assignments. R6 protected for couples/private/matrimonial. R7/R9/R10 are NOT inherently forbidden: they are normally held by operator bookings; if an operator block covers them they are unavailable, but if released/available they may be assigned. Operator blocks are authoritative. Operator-room release is a supported function (existing Operator Room Release workflow / planned flow).

rooms._note: Cami 2026 room map — guidance only; Airtable Rooms table is source of truth. guest_assignable reflects whether the bot may auto-place a guest; operator_block_dependent rooms are assignable only when no operator block covers the dates.

R3.note: beds joined = matrimonial couple room; otherwise mixed

R6.note: couples/private/matrimonial only; do not place unrelated guests

R7.note: assignable only when not covered by an operator block

R9.note: assignable only when not covered by an operator block

R10.note: assignable only when not covered by an operator block

packages._note: 3x.2f: 2026 price table CONFIRMED by owner. Prices are PER PERSON for the 7-night package in a SHARED room. Double room = +€10 per night per person. Shorter/longer stays: prorate accommodation per night + add-ons per day. Dec/Jan/Feb closed.

double._note: +€10 per night per person on top of base

seasons_2026._note: month -> season. 3x.2e: Mar shoulder; Jul+Sep high; Aug peak; Nov+Dec/Jan/Feb CLOSED (no bookings).

non_7_night_pricing._note: 3x.2g (Ale, CONFIRMED): for stays shorter or longer than 7 nights, take the CHOSEN package's seasonal total, divide by 7, round UP to the nearest €5 to get the per-night price, then multiply by the number of nights. This supersedes the earlier provisional 'Malibu base + add-ons per day' guess. A custom/short stay also uses the €100 deposit tier (see payment.deposit_rule).
non_7_night_pricing._addon_days_note: a guest can add gear/lessons for as many days as they have remaining in their booking — cannot exceed nights booked.
non_7_night_pricing.example: Uluwatu shoulder (€349), 4 nights: per_night = round_up_5(349/7=49.86)=50; total = 50*4 = €200 per person; any rentals/lessons added per day at service_catalog rates.

recommendation_map._note: 3x.2e (owner): do NOT ask the guest's skill level. Ask what they WANT (wetsuit / board / lessons) and map to the package that bundles it; or build a custom stay from accommodation + add-ons.

handoff._note: 3x.2c: trigger list confirmed; added paid-cancellation, non-safe reschedule, and any-unanswerable-question triggers. Handoff is a product feature, not just a failure state — short-term + future Staff UI design in docs/STAFF-HANDOFF-PLAN.md. Channel/notification destination still owner_required.
handoff._handoff_channel_note: 3x.2d: start with a WhatsApp message to Cami. The target number is EDITABLE in config so other clients can point elsewhere. Single destination for now (no topic routing yet).

handoff_whatsapp_target.label: Cami (TEST = operator number)
handoff_whatsapp_target._note: 3x.2e: number lives in the secret file (currently operator's number as a stand-in for Cami). Edit secret file to change for production. Per-client editable.

handoff._tell_guest_before_handoff_note: Default assumption: bot says it will check with the team before handing off. Confirm wording with owner.
handoff.handoff_safe_reply: Let me connect you with our team — they'll be with you shortly.

llm_safety._note: LLM safety rules are confirmed architectural requirements. Not negotiable.

customer_memory_privacy._note: No Layer-2 personal data to PG until all privacy gate items are checked. Raw exports stay off-repo. Planning (3x.2) may proceed; data import requires gate.

service_addons._note: 3x.2c: owner wants guests to text the bot DURING their stay to add services (dinner, wetsuit, surfboard, surf lesson, etc.) with a number of days/quantity. Bot creates a Stripe payment link; after webhook-confirmed payment, a voucher is issued that the guest shows to staff ON-SITE to verify and redeem. Plan + data model in docs/DURING-STAY-ADDONS-PLAN.md. No runtime yet. Reuses the proven Stripe link + webhook payment-truth spine (NOT a new payment integration). Pricing, scheduling, and weather rules are owner_required.

service_catalog._note: 3x.2g (Ale, CONFIRMED): per-day rental + per-lesson prices. Two engine-default pricing mechanisms are used here so any client can express the same shapes: (1) `pricing_model: tiered_by_quantity` for items that get cheaper when you buy several (lessons); (2) bundles (see `bundles` below) for gear combos with their own price. Edit price_eur values here to change prices.

surf_lesson._pricing_model_note: 3x.2g (Ale): one lesson €35; if booking more than one lesson, €30 each.

dinner_meal._note: Not re-confirmed by Ale in 3x.2g; remains provisional. Guests book dinners during stay and show the booking message / payment to staff when collecting food.

yoga_class._note: 3x.2g (Ale, CONFIRMED): €15 per class, booked ON SITE. Yoga is an extra UNLESS it is part of a special camp (e.g. a Log & Yoga retreat / special camp) where it may be included — see special_camps.

bundles._note: 3x.2g (Ale, CONFIRMED): gear combos priced as a bundle. In both promos the wetsuit is effectively included free with the board. Engine-default bundle mechanism: a bundle has its own price that may be lower than the sum of parts.

wetsuit_plus_softtop._note: promo: same price as soft top alone — wetsuit free

wetsuit_plus_hardboard._note: promo: same price as hard board alone — wetsuit free

special_camps._note: 3x.2g (Ale): yoga is normally an extra, but during special camps (e.g. a Log & Yoga retreat) it may be bundled in. Special camps are staff-defined; bot does not invent them — handoff for special-camp requests.

lesson_scheduling._note: 3x.2g (Ale, CONFIRMED): lessons run almost every day except low season; tracked MANUALLY by staff. Engine-default: bot collects the lesson request + paid voucher; STAFF assign the actual time slot. Bot does not auto-book a slot.

bad_weather_or_no_waves_policy._note: 3x.2g (Ale, CONFIRMED): if it is really flat / very bad weather and the guest doesn't feel like surfing, staff refund the LESSON. Decided day by day by staff — not an automatic bot action.

staff_queries._note: 3x.2c: staff want to ask the bot read-only operational questions ('who has a surfboard today?', 'who has dinner tonight?', 'who arrives today?'). Plan in docs/STAFF-QUERY-ASSISTANT-PLAN.md. Read-only, staff-channel only, never exposed to guests. LLM maps NL -> fixed safe parameterized intents; NEVER generates arbitrary SQL. No mutation on this path ever.

staff_directory._note: 3x.2e: roles model. MASTER ADMIN numbers can manage the directory and run admin tasks via the bot (add/remove staff numbers, etc.). Approved STAFF numbers get staff-channel features (queries, handoff replies, redemptions). A number in neither list is treated as a guest. This allowlist gates every staff-only capability. Interim store = config/Airtable; management PORTAL = Stage 6 Staff UI.

master_admin._note: 3x.2e: MASTER ADMIN numbers can add/remove staff numbers and run admin tasks via the bot. The actual numbers live in the secret file (currently the operator number as test admin). Default permissions below apply to admins.

admin_via_whatsapp._note: 3x.2e: a MASTER ADMIN number can manage approved staff numbers and run admin tasks by messaging the bot. Primary auth = sender is in master_admin.numbers_ref. Optional second factor = the secret password. Every change is logged and the session is time-boxed.

operations._note: 3x.2d: check-out 11:00, check-in 15:00 (may change later — easy to edit). Housekeeping: a room freed by checkout must be cleaned; cleaning window is checkout -> next check-in (11:00 -> 15:00 on turnover). A freed room with no next guest STILL needs cleaning the same day. Cleaning buffer not specified -> owner_required.

stage4_entry_gate._note: Stage 4 may begin when all dangerous action rules have confirmed, provisional, or handoff_only classification — no null rule without an explicit safe default.
